pi_items.source: Confirmed by setup_task.sh which explicitly creates and configures this directory for the task.

metadata_value: equatorial_to_galactic: NGP declination in degrees not converted to radians
equatorial_to_horizontal: atan2 arguments swapped for azimuth
angular_separation: cosine term uses addition instead of subtraction
hms_to_degrees: seconds divided by 60 instead of 3600
verified_value: equatorial_to_galactic: NGP declination in degrees not converted to radians
equatorial_to_horizontal: atan2 arguments swapped for azimuth
angular_separation: cosine term uses addition instead of subtraction
hms_to_degrees: seconds divided by 60 instead of 3600
pi_items.source: Manually inspected the injected python scripts in setup_task.sh and evaluated the mathematical formulas. All 4 documented bugs are intentionally implemented exactly as described.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on completely synthetic code and data generated during task setup. All four described mathematical bugs are accurately injected into the source files. However, there is a bug in the verification script (export_result.sh): the No Regression check requires a test named test_galactic_to_equatorial_vega to pass, but this test is never created by setup_task.sh. As agents are instructed not to modify test files, they cannot earn the 10 No Regression points, limiting their maximum possible score to 90/100. Despite this, agents can still pass the 65-point threshold by fixing all four bugs.
